content.problemStatement:
Chef is given two strings and wants to make them **exactly identical** using delete operations.

In one operation, Chef can delete **exactly one character** from **either** of the two strings.

Chef wants to determine the **minimum number of delete operations** required to make both strings the same.

Function DeclarationFunction NameminimumDeleteSteps\texttt{minimumDeleteSteps}minimumDeleteSteps

Parameters
a\texttt{a}a (string):
b\texttt{b}b (string):
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the **minimum number of delete operations** required to make both strings identical.
The returned value is a **non-negative integer**.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \le T \le 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣a∣,∣b∣≤5001 \le |\texttt{a}|, |\texttt{b}| \le 5001≤∣a∣,∣b∣≤500
Both a\texttt{a}a and b\texttt{b}b consist only of lowercase English letters.
Input Format
The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of **two lines**:

The first line contains the string a\texttt{a}a.
The second line contains the string b\texttt{b}b.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single integer: 

the minimum number of delete operations Chef needs to perform to make both strings identical.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
abcde
ace
hello
world
aabbcc
abcabc
sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1
LCS of "abcde"\texttt{"abcde"}"abcde" and "ace"\texttt{"ace"}"ace" is "ace"\texttt{"ace"}"ace" (length = 3).
Minimum deletions required:(5 - 3) + (3 - 3) = 2


Test Case 2
LCS of "hello"\texttt{"hello"}"hello" and "world"\texttt{"world"}"world" is "o"\texttt{"o"}"o" (length = 1).
Minimum deletions required:(5 - 1) + (5 - 1) = 8


Test Case 3
One possible LCS of "aabbcc"\texttt{"aabbcc"}"aabbcc" and "abcabc"\texttt{"abcabc"}"abcabc" is "abbc"\texttt{"abbc"}"abbc" (length = 4).
Minimum deletions required:
(6 - 4) + (6 - 4) = 4

hidden.input:
3
abcde
ace
hello
world
aabbcc
abcabc
